content.full_text:
Farn Porrrrcel PRACrrcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
September 27,2001
Garland S. Rosauro, Political Director
Operating Engineers Local Union No. 3
1620 South Loop Road
Alameda, CA94502-7090
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-205
Dear Mr. Rosauro
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your request does
not refer to specific facts and dates, we provide informal assistance. Informal assistance
does not confer immunity under Section 831 14. (Regulation 18329(c).)
QUESTTON
When and how should a union report an enforceable promise to provide printing
goods and services to a politicalparty?
CONCLUSION
An enforceable promise to provide printing goods and services to a political party
is a contribution. The contribution is made at the time the union enters into the written
agreement to provide the goods and services, and the party expends funds or enters into a
contract with a third party in reliance on the promise. Depending on when the
contribution is made, it would be reportable in the union's next required campaign
statement on Form 460, Schedule D.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9-1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
t::


File No. I-01-205
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are making a request for advice on behalf of the Operating Engineers, Local
Union No. 3 ("union"). The union owns and operates a printing press. The California
Democratic Parfy has requested that the union provide printing services in connection
with elections to be held next year. The printing services would include paper, staff to
operate the presses and related binding services. The precise nature of what is to be
printed has not yet been determined. The Democratic Party will provide you with the
graphics and artwork for the printing, and would have sole discretion over the content of
the printed materials. The fair market value of the printing will not exceed $20,000.00.
The DemocraticParty has requested that the union place in writing its promise to
provide the printing services. The DemocraticParty advises thatit intends to make
payments to other vendors, (e.g., mail houses) now in reliance on this promise. In effect,
the Democratic Party wants an enforceable contract with the union to ensure that it
provides the services at a future date. You would like to know how and when to report
this promise of printing services to the California DemocraticParty.
ANALYSIS
Pursuant to $ 82015, Regulation 18216 states in pertinent part:
"(a) A person who makes an enforceable promise to make a
payment to or at the behest of a candidate or committee makes a
contribution to the candidate or committee, except to the extent the
person receives full and adequate consideration, and unless it is
clear from the surrounding circumstances that the enforceable
promise is not made for political purposes.
"(b) A person makes an 'enforceable promise to make a payment,'
as that term is used in subsection (a), if he or she:
"1T...fl
"(7) Promises in writing to make a payment for specific goods or
services, and the candidate or committee, based on the promise,
expends specific funds or enters into an enforceable contract with a
third party."
Presumably, the Democratic Party will not pay "full and adequate consideration"
to the union for the printing goods and services in question. The party will be entering
into third party contracts based upon the union's promise. Therefore, the union's written
promise to provide printing goods and services for political purposes to the Democratic
Party is a contribution. (See also $ 82015.) The contribution is made at the time the union
enters into the agreement, and the party expends funds or enters into a contract with a


File No. l-01-205
Page No. 3
third party in reliance on the promise. (Bagatelos Advice Letter, No. I-95-248.)
Depending on when the contribution is made, the union would be required to report it on
its next requ^ired campaign statement (semi-annual or pre-election) on Form 460,
Schedule p' ($$ 84200,84200.5,84211(k)(5).) If th; exact value of the contribution is
not known atthattime, the union may provide a good faith estimate for reporting
purposes.' (Bagatelos Advice Letter, supra, No. i-OS-Z+S.;
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By: William L. Williams, Jr.
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

its political action committee.
' If flr. contribution is reported as an estimate, the union must amend the campaigrr statement
'when the exact amount of the contribution is known.

statement on Form 460, Schedule D.
